concepts.title: Properties of diameter of parabola
concepts.conceptBody:
Diameter of a parabola

Definition : Diameter of a conic is the locus of middle points of a series of its parallel chords.

 

Equation of diameter of a parabola : 

Let the parabola be ${{\text{y}}^{2}}=4\text{ax}$ ……(1)

Let $\text{AB}$ be one of the chords of a series of parallel chords

having slope $\text{m}$.

Let $\text{P}\left( \alpha ,\beta \right)$ be the middle point of chord $\text{AB}$, then equation

of $AB$ will be $T={{S}_{1}}$

Or $\text{y}\beta -2\text{a}\left( \text{x}+\alpha \right)={{\beta }^{2}}-4\text{a}\alpha$ ……(2)

$\text{ Slope }\text{ of }\text{ line }\left( 2 \right)=\frac{2\text{a}}{\beta }$

But slope of line (1) i.e., line $\text{AB}$ is $\text{m}$.

$\therefore \frac{2\text{a}}{\beta }=\text{m }\text{ or }\beta =\frac{2\text{a}}{\text{m}}$

Hence locus of $\text{P}\left( \alpha ,\beta \right)$ i.e., equation of diameter (which is the locus of a series of

parallel chords having slope $\text{m}$ ) is

$y=\frac{2a}{m}$ …….(3)

Clearly line (3) is parallel to the axis of the parabola.

Thus a diameter of a parabola is parallel to its axis.

Note : 1. The point is which any diameter meets the curve is called the extremity of the diameter.

2. Any line which is parallel to the axis of the parabola drawn through any point on

the parabola is called diameter and its equation is y-co-ordinate of that point. If

point on diameter $\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$, then diameter is $\text{y}={{\text{y}}_{1}}$.

Remark 1. The tangent at the extremity of a diameter of a parabola is parallel to 

the system of chords it bisects. 

Let $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$ (c variable) represents the system of parallel chords, then the

equation of the diameter of

is $\begin{matrix} {{y}^{2}}=4ax \\ y=\frac{2a}{m} \\ \end{matrix}$

The diameter meets the parabola ${{\text{y}}^{2}}=4\text{ax}$ at $\left( \frac{\text{a}}{{{\text{m}}^{2}}},\frac{2\text{a}}{\text{m}} \right)$ and tangent is

$\text{y}=\text{mx}+\frac{\text{a}}{\text{m}}$ which is parallel to $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$.

Remark 2 : Tangents at the end of any chord meet on the diameter which 

bisects the chords. 

If extremities of the chord be $\text{P}\left( \text{at}_{1}^{2},2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{1}} \right)$ and $\text{Q}\left( \text{att}_{2}^{2},2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{2}} \right)$ then its slope

$\text{m}=\frac{2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{2}}-2\text{at}}{\text{at}_{2}^{2}-\text{at}_{1}^{2}}\Rightarrow \text{m}=\frac{2}{\left( {{\text{t}}_{2}}+{{\text{t}}_{1}} \right)}$

$\therefore $ Equation of diameter is

$\text{y}=2\text{a}/\text{m}\Rightarrow \text{y}=\text{a}\left( {{\text{t}}_{1}}+{{\text{t}}_{2}} \right)$

Now tangents at $\text{P}\left( \text{at}_{1}^{2},2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{1}} \right)$ and $\text{Q}\left( \text{att}_{2}^{2},2a{{\text{t}}_{2}} \right)$ meet at a point $\left[ a{{t}_{1}}{{\text{t}}_{2}},\text{a}\left( {{\text{t}}_{1}}+{{\text{t}}_{2}} \right) \right]$ which lies on (1).

Alternative Method : Let equation of any chord PQ be $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$

If tangents at $\text{P}$ and $\text{Q}$ meet at $\text{R}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ then $\text{PQ}$ is the chord of contact with

respect to $\text{R}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$.

$\therefore $ Equation of $\text{PQ}$ is

$\text{y}{{\text{y}}_{1}}=2\text{x}\left( \text{x}+{{\text{x}}_{1}} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ y}=\frac{2\text{a}}{{{\text{y}}_{1}}}\text{x}+\frac{2\text{a}{{\text{x}}_{1}}}{{{\text{y}}_{1}}}$

which is identical to $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$

$\text{m}=\frac{2\text{a}}{{{\text{y}}_{1}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{y}}_{1}}=\frac{2\text{a}}{\text{m}}$

Hence locus of $R\left( {{x}_{1}},{{y}_{1}} \right)$ is $y=\frac{2a}{m}$ which bisects the chord $PQ$.

Note : The quantity $4\text{acose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta $ is called the parameter of the diameter AQ. It is

equal to length of the chord which is parallel to AT and passes through the focus.

i.e., $\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} a\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta & ~=a\left( 1+\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{2}}\theta \right)=a+a\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{2}}\theta \\ {} & ~=a+\frac{a}{{{m}^{2}}} \\ {} & ~=a+VL=SP \\ \end{array}$

but length of focal chord if $\text{P}\left( \text{a}{{\text{t}}^{2}},2\text{at} \right)$ is $\left\{ .\text{S}\left( \text{a},0 \right) \right\}$

$\text{a}{{\left( \text{t}+\frac{1}{\text{t}} \right)}^{2}}$

$\text{tan}\theta =\frac{2\text{at}-0}{\text{a}{{\text{t}}^{2}}-\text{a}}=\frac{2\text{t}}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}-1}$

Or $t-\frac{1}{t}=2\,\cot \,\theta $

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \text{a}{{\left( \text{t}+\frac{1}{\text{t}} \right)}^{2}} & ~=\text{a}\left\{ {{\left( \text{t}-\frac{1}{\text{t}} \right)}^{2}}+4 \right\}=\text{a}\left\{ {{(2\text{cot}\theta )}^{2}}+4 \right\} \\ {} & ~=4\text{acose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \\ {} & ~=4\text{SP} \\ \end{array}$

examples.title: Equation of diameter of parabola $y^2=x$ corresponding to the chord $x-y+1=0$ is
